message:
feat(bench): add backdrop apply cost benchmarks (bd-l8x9.9.1)

- Add bench_backdrop_apply_cost group to visual_fx_bench.rs
- Measures raw cell write vs SolidFx backdrop render
- Tests with/without scrim and opacity variations
- Performance budgets: 80x24 <100μs, 120x40 <250μs, 240x80 <1ms

Also includes:
- Input fairness guard type alias for FairnessEventType compatibility
- Clipboard event handling in fairness classification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Golden Output Harness + Checksums
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)

- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2","title":"Alien Artifact: Formal Resize Scheduler Model","description":"## Goal
Design a mathematically principled resize scheduler (control theory + change-point detection + anytime-valid testing).

## Scope
- Formal model of resize event stream with uncertainty.
- Decision rules for when to coalesce vs render immediately.
- Explicit failure modes and safeguards.

## Deliverables
- Model spec with assumptions/priors and decision thresholds.
- Pseudocode for scheduler + evidence ledger.

## Tests / Validation
- Property tests for decision rule invariants.
- Simulation harness for synthetic resize streams.
- E2E logs include scheduler decisions and confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scheduler is explainable and reproducible.
- Decision thresholds are defensible and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.885261170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297481942Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:58:02.297460572Z","close_reason":"Completed spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":331,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien Artifact Math Notes (MANDATORY)

Purpose
- Provide a mathematically principled resize scheduler with anytime validity, explicit loss tradeoffs, and provable bounds on worst‑case behavior.

Core Model
- Treat resize events as a non‑stationary time series with regime shifts.
- Objective: minimize expected loss L(action, state) with asymmetric costs.

Loss Matrix (example)
- keep_reflow | skip_frame
  - smooth (true)  : 0   |  30
  - storm (burst)  : 10  |  1
- Intent: during bursts, skipping a frame is cheap; during steady state, skipping is costly.

Decision Rule (Bayes)
- Maintain posterior over regimes:
  P(storm|data_t) ∝ P(storm) × Π P(x_i|storm)
- Action a* = argmin_a Σ_c L(a,c) × P(c|data_t)

Anytime Validity (e‑process)
- Define e‑value e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Trigger “storm mode” when e_t ≥ 1/α (optional stopping safe).
- This lets the scheduler switch modes without invalidating guarantees.

Output Guarantees
- Bound expected latency under steady state.
- Bound average render cost under storms by throttling to max_fps.
- Explicitly log when bounds are violated.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:02Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","title":"Auto UI Height Measurement Pass","description":"## Task
Implement auto UI height measurement pass with caching to avoid repeated cost.

## Scope
- Measure UI content height once per stable layout.
- Cache result and invalidate on layout changes.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: cache hit/miss behavior.
- PTY E2E: resize + content changes with JSONL logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Auto height is stable and does not flicker.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.801083132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:09:59.519103507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":341,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: fixed inline auto height cache bug (min-height measurements were never cached).  now always caches  and only resets diff/scroll region when effective height changes. ## Acceptance Criteria

- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-1rzr","title":"Action Timeline: Alert Threshold Calibration (Conformal + e-process)","description":"## Goal
Set change-point alert thresholds with formal false-positive control and provide an explanation ledger per alert.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Conformal thresholding on residuals from the sketch baseline:
- Given calibration residuals R = {r_1..r_n}, threshold q = quantile_{1-alpha} using (n+1) rule.
- Alert when r_t > q.

Anytime-valid layer:
- Maintain an e-process on residuals to allow early alerts without inflating false positives.
- Example: e_t = exp(lambda * (r_t - mu) - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2), E_t = product e_t.
- Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- FPR <= alpha on synthetic and recorded traces.
- Alert carries evidence ledger: residual, q, conformal score, e-value, alpha.
- No alert spam under steady-state traffic.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_conformal_threshold: threshold equals (1-alpha) quantile of calibration set.
- unit_evalue_monotone: e-process nonnegative and grows with evidence.
- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Replay trace with known change points; log alert timing and evidence.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

- GRAPA adaptive lambda support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-vv66","title":"Comprehensive Unit Test Suite with Detailed Logging","description":"# Comprehensive Unit Test Suite with Detailed Logging

## Background
Plan Section 13.1 requires property tests and unit tests for all kernel components. The user specifically requested "comprehensive unit tests and e2e test scripts with great, detailed logging so we can be sure that everything is working perfectly after implementation."

## Scope
Every module in ftui must have a corresponding test module with:
1. Unit tests for all public APIs
2. Property tests for invariant preservation
3. Detailed logging of test execution for debugging

## Test Logging Requirements
All tests MUST use structured logging (tracing) with:
- Test name and module path
- Input parameters (for property tests, show the generated inputs)
- Expected vs actual values on failure
- Timing information for performance-sensitive code
- Stack traces on failure

## Per-Module Test Coverage (MANDATORY)

### ftui-core Tests
- [ ] TerminalSession: enter/exit raw mode, panic cleanup, mode tracking
- [ ] TerminalCapabilities: detection from environment variables
- [ ] Event types: serialization, comparison, conversion from backend

### ftui-render Tests
- [ ] Cell: 16-byte layout assertion, bits_eq correctness
- [ ] CellContent: char encoding, grapheme encoding, width calculation
- [ ] PackedRgba: RGBA packing, Porter-Duff over operation, opacity
- [ ] CellAttrs: flag packing, link ID encoding
- [ ] Buffer: bounds checking, scissor stack invariants, opacity stack invariants
- [ ] GraphemePool: intern/lookup, refcount correctness, slot reuse
- [ ] LinkRegistry: register/lookup, deduplication
- [ ] BufferDiff: change detection, run grouping correctness
- [ ] Presenter: ANSI output correctness, style tracking, link handling

### ftui-style Tests
- [ ] Style: merge semantics, mask handling, property inheritance
- [ ] Color: parsing, downgrade (truecolor→256→16→mono)
- [ ] Theme: slot resolution, preset correctness

### ftui-text Tests
- [ ] Text/Span: construction, styling, width measurement
- [ ] Wrapping: word wrap, character wrap, no mid-grapheme breaks
- [ ] Truncation: ellipsis placement, width correctness
- [ ] Measurement: min/max width calculation

### ftui-layout Tests
- [ ] Rect: intersection, containment, inner margin
- [ ] Sides: CSS-like tuple conversion
- [ ] Layout algorithms: flex, grid constraints

### ftui-runtime Tests
- [ ] Program: event dispatch, command handling
- [ ] Cmd: batch, sequence, tick scheduling
- [ ] Simulator: deterministic replay, snapshot capture

## Test Harness Requirements

### Logging Setup
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use tracing::{info, debug, error};
    use tracing_test::traced_test;
    
    #[traced_test]
    #[test]
    fn test_cell_bits_eq() {
        info\!("Testing Cell::bits_eq with identical cells");
        let cell_a = Cell::new('A', Style::default());
        let cell_b = Cell::new('A', Style::default());
        debug\!(cell_a = ?cell_a, cell_b = ?cell_b, "Comparing cells");
        assert\!(cell_a.bits_eq(&cell_b), "Identical cells should be equal");
        info\!("Cell bits_eq test passed");
    }
}
```

### Property Test Logging
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
proptest\! {
    #[test]
    fn buffer_diff_roundtrip(buffer1 in arb_buffer(80, 24), buffer2 in arb_buffer(80, 24)) {
        info\!(width = 80, height = 24, "Property test: buffer diff roundtrip");
        debug\!(buffer1_hash = ?hash(&buffer1), buffer2_hash = ?hash(&buffer2), "Input buffers");
        
        let diff = BufferDiff::compute(&buffer1, &buffer2);
        info\!(change_count = diff.len(), "Diff computed");
        
        // Apply diff and verify
        let mut result = buffer1.clone();
        apply_diff(&mut result, &buffer2, &diff);
        
        for y in 0..24 {
            for x in 0..80 {
                prop_assert_eq\!(
                    result.get(x, y), buffer2.get(x, y),
                    "Cell mismatch at ({}, {})", x, y
                );
            }
        }
        info\!("Property test passed");
    }
}
```

## Test Categories with Counts

| Module | Min Test Count | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| Cell/CellContent | 15+ | Layout, encoding, width |
| Buffer | 20+ | Bounds, scissor, opacity, clear |
| GraphemePool | 10+ | Intern, refcount, reuse |
| BufferDiff | 15+ | Detection, runs, edge cases |
| Presenter | 25+ | All ANSI sequences, state tracking |
| InputParser | 30+ | All key codes, mouse, paste |
| Style | 15+ | Merge, mask, inheritance |
| Text | 20+ | Width, wrap, truncate |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every public API has at least one test
- [ ] Property tests cover all invariants from plan Section 2.3
- [ ] All tests use tracing for logging
- [ ] Test output includes timing information
- [ ] cargo test --all produces clear, structured output
- [ ] Test coverage > 80% for kernel crates

## Dependencies
- Requires: bd-10i.3 (Phase 1 types exist)
- Blocks: bd-2gx (Quality Gates)

## Test Framework Stack
- Unit tests: built-in #[test]
- Property tests: proptest
- Logging: tracing + tracing-test
- Coverage: cargo-llvm-cov
- Timing: criterion for perf-sensitive

## CI Requirements
- All tests run on every PR
- Test failure shows full log output
- Coverage report generated
- Test timing tracked for regression","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:22:11.213562541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:51:58.207670537Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:51:58.207585917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vv66","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vv66","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vv66","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":558,"issue_id":"bd-vv66","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Assessed workspace test coverage: 2,145 tests passing across all crates. Added 9 GraphemePool GC tests (mark-and-sweep correctness). Added 22 Align widget tests, 9 Group widget tests. Key coverage: ftui-core 273, ftui-render 556, ftui-text 306, ftui-style 113, ftui-layout 75, ftui-runtime 159, ftui-widgets 562. All mandatory modules have tests above minimum counts. Property tests exist for all kernel data structures. GC coverage was the main gap — now filled.","created_at":"2026-02-02T01:33:18Z"},{"id":559,"issue_id":"bd-vv66","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Assessment: Workspace now has 2200+ tests across all crates. Key coverage: ftui-render (560 unit + property tests), ftui-text (300+ with unicode width corpus of 1000+ cases), ftui-widgets (306 tests), ftui-extras (476+ tests), ftui-pty (39 tests), ftui-core (273 tests). E2E tests: 16 tests across inline/cleanup/altscreen/input suites with structured JSON logging. Quality gates verified (bd-2gx). Closing.","created_at":"2026-02-02T01:51:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vzjn","title":"Gradients: LUT + Memoization + SIMD Hot Path","description":"## Goal
Make gradient sampling effectively free at render time using precomputed lookup tables and tight hot paths.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization)
- Precompute t values per row/col and store in SmallVec or Vec<u16> (fixed-point).
- Precompute gradient samples into a LUT keyed by (gradient_id, size, mode).
- Use SIMD-friendly loops for applying sampled colors to glyph runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Per-character gradient sampling is O(1) with no trig or division in hot path.
- LUT reuse across frames when size/gradient unchanged.
- No allocations during steady-state rendering.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_lut_reuse_same_size: cache hit for same dimensions.
- unit_lut_invalidate_resize: cache miss on size change.
- unit_fixed_point_accuracy: fixed-point t within epsilon of float.
- perf_gradient_hotpath: measure p50/p95 vs baseline; include logs.

## Isomorphism Proof
- Output colors identical to reference float implementation (golden checksums).

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
